description:
Pre-existing ship-blocker surfaced by mutagen-hcs.7's audit (2026-06-23). NOT introduced by .7 — the crashing line is byte-identical at base 0926844.

## Bug
`mix mutagen --stream` hard-crashes with a FunctionClauseError before emitting any NDJSON line. Root cause: lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (maybe_emit_stream_end / the stream-end envelope) calls `Map.from_struct(report.meta || %{})`, but `report.meta` is a PLAIN MAP — base_report builds `meta: %{...}` and the Report type is `meta: map() | nil`; there is NO Report.Meta struct. So `Map.from_struct/1` raises on every `--stream` invocation (reproduced at both --max-concurrency 1 and >1). The progress/stream composition is correct but never reached.

This advertises a flag that crashes on first use — exactly the kind of embarrassing-first-release gap this epic exists to prevent.

## Why it was hidden
test/mutagen_ex/json_streamer_test.exs only calls `MutagenEx.JsonStreamer.emit_start/3` (and friends) directly with a hand-built plain map; the real Mix-task `--stream` CLI path has ZERO integration coverage.

## Fix (in scope)
- Replace `Map.from_struct(report.meta || %{})` with `(report.meta || %{})` (meta is already a plain map) — or whatever the stream-end envelope actually needs. Verify the full stream-start + per-site + stream-end envelope sequence emits valid NDJSON.
- Add a REAL `--stream` CLI integration test: drive `mix mutagen --stream ...` (subprocess or Mix.Task) on a tiny scope, assert it exits 0 and emits well-formed line-delimited JSON envelopes (start/result/end) with the documented kinds, AND that it coexists with progress (progress on stderr, NDJSON on the json sink). This closes the coverage gap that hid the bug.

## Out of scope
- Do not change the NDJSON schema/kinds themselves (only fix the crash + add coverage).
- Do not touch telemetry (removed in .7) or the package files: whitelist (.1).

## Acceptance
- `mix mutagen --stream` on a real scope exits 0 and emits valid NDJSON (no FunctionClauseError).
- New CLI-level --stream integration test added and green.
- `mix test` + `make smoke` green.

comments.text:
IMPLEMENTED (commit 0b48d60)

Root cause: lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex stream-start path called Map.from_struct(report.meta || %{}); report.meta is a plain map (no Report.Meta struct), so Map.from_struct/1 raised FunctionClauseError on every --stream run before any NDJSON line. Fix: pass (report.meta || %{}) directly — it already carries the atom keys JsonStreamer.meta_to_wire/1 reads. NDJSON schema/kinds unchanged.

Coverage: new CLI --stream integration test test/integration/stream_cli_test.exs boots a tmp Mix project (path: dep), drives mix mutagen --stream via System.cmd, asserts exit 0 + well-formed NDJSON (start -> result/compile_error+ -> end envelopes, each version 1 with kind discriminator) on stdout, and no NDJSON leakage onto stderr (two-sink separation intact). Tagged :stream_integration (excluded by default in test_helper, wired into mix test.integration).

Regression-gate verified: stashing the one-line fix makes the new test fail with the exact Map.from_struct/1 FunctionClauseError at mutagen.ex:868; restoring it passes.

Acceptance:
- mix mutagen --stream on a real scope exits 0 with valid NDJSON (no FunctionClauseError) — verified via the new integration test.
- New CLI --stream integration test added + green.
- make smoke green: 643 tests, 0 failures.
- mix test.integration (stream + downstream) green: 4 tests, 0 failures.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: agent-averify-hcs8-b7a25825be76e265 (parent 033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3)
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3/subagents/agent-averify-hcs8-b7a25825be76e265.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 4, all within scope. lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex (1-line fix), test/integration/stream_cli_test.exs (+272, new test), test/test_helper.exs (+8, :stream_integration exclusion tag + doc), mix.exs (alias-only: added `--include stream_integration` to the test.integration alias; NOT a deps/package change). No strays.

Semantic checks:
- Fix correct (meta serialized right, NDJSON schema unchanged): yes. report.meta is built at mutagen.ex:959-963 as a plain atom-keyed map (%{tool_version, elixir_version, otp_version, exunit_seed}); Report.meta type is `map() | nil` with no Meta struct, so Map.from_struct/1 was guaranteed to raise. JsonStreamer.meta_to_wire/1 (json_streamer.ex:110) reads those atom keys via Map.get — passing (report.meta || %{}) directly feeds it exactly that. meta_to_wire/1 is byte-identical between JsonStreamer and JsonReporter, and in my live run the start envelope's meta {"elixir_version":"1.19.5","exunit_seed":0,"otp_version":"28","tool_version":"0.1.0"} matched the aggregate document's meta exactly. emit_start still wraps through meta_to_wire — schema/kinds unchanged. Pipe precedence is correct ((expr) |> Map.put_new).
- `mix mutagen --stream` exits 0 + valid NDJSON: yes. New integration test drives `mix mutagen --stream --scope lib/<app>/calc.ex --tests ...` via System.cmd on a fresh tmp path:-dep project; my run produced start -> result(+) -> end on stdout, each a parseable object with kind discriminator and version "1", positive total, meta map present, end aborted:false. (My direct manual run on lib/mutagen_ex/progress.ex correctly hit self_mutation_refused -> exit 2 / aborted-end with 0 sites, which is why the test uses a downstream tmp project to get real per-site results.)
- Regression-gate (FAILS without fix, PASSES with it): CONFIRMED both ways. With fix: 1 test, 0 failures. Reverted ONLY the one line (Map.from_struct(report.meta || %{})): 1 test, 1 failure — `** (FunctionClauseError) no function clause matching in Map.from_struct/1 ... %{elixir_version: ..., otp_version: ..., tool_version: ..., exunit_seed: 0} ... lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:868`, failing the `assert mutagen_code == 0`. Exact documented bug. Restored; working tree clean against HEAD.
- Progress + --stream coexist (two-sink separation): yes. Test redirects child stderr to a file and asserts no NDJSON (no JSON-object-with-kind line) leaks onto stderr while the stream lands on stdout (stream_emitted_on_stderr?/1 refute). Passed. (Child stderr is a pipe so progress :auto gates off; .7's in-process __build_progress_reporter__ unit tests cover the TTY path.)
- make smoke / mix test.integration: smoke green (643 tests, 0 failures, 23 excluded; no ast flake, seed-0 not needed). mix test.integration green (4 tests, 0 failures) — Including tags now lists :stream_integration. archive_integration passed here (this stage is based on 3420a93, not main).

Verdict: PASS

Notes (advisory only): Pre-existing benign warning unrelated to this change — test/integration/support/self_mutation_harness.exs doesn't match test_load_filters. No action needed.

AUDIT: APPROVE (auto — selective audit)
Session: 033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3
Log: ~/.claude-remembers/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/033814be-550f-48f5-ab4a-ac141d9e94a3.jsonl
Reasoning: VERIFY PASS with a PROVEN falsifiable regression gate — verifier reverted the one-line fix and reproduced the exact FunctionClauseError at mutagen.ex:868, restored it and the new CLI --stream integration test passed. Fix correct (report.meta plain map fed directly to meta_to_wire; start-envelope meta matches the aggregate document; NDJSON schema/kinds unchanged). --stream exits 0 with valid NDJSON; two-sink separation (no NDJSON on stderr) intact; smoke 643/0 + mix test.integration 4/0 (now includes :stream_integration). Scope clean (mutagen.ex fix + new test + test_helper tag + mix.exs alias-only). No judgment call remaining.

APPROVED (auto). Rebased onto epic (clean), merged --ff-only. Final gates green: mix test --seed 0 643/0, format clean, deps.unlock clean, mix test.integration 4/0 (stream + archive + downstream + self_mutation). Closing.

title: Fix mix mutagen --stream crash (Map.from_struct on plain map) + add CLI streaming integration test
